commands.review_summary:
This PR narrows the database-first legacy-store guard so it skips three generated browser bundles while keeping ordinary runtime assets/dist files in scope and updating the focused test.

PR surface: Tests +29, Other +14. Total +43 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: build asset hooks create the three browser bundle paths under `extensions`, while current main scans all `.js` files under `extensions`. I did not run the build or guard locally because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed; the patch is focused and the remaining action is normal maintainer review plus clean CI for a protected maintainer PR.

target.title: fix(ci): skip generated bundles in database guard
target.body:
## Summary

- exclude only the three browser bundles written by the diffs and Canvas build scripts from the database-first legacy-store guard
- retain scanning for ordinary runtime source, including regular `assets` and `dist` paths
- prevent the native release prepare gate from parsing large minified client bundles after `pnpm build`

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/check-database-first-legacy-stores.test.ts`
- Testbox `tbx_01kv9rcrfm454tztvtb6q2xyn9`: `corepack pnpm build`
- Testbox `tbx_01kv9rcrfm454tztvtb6q2xyn9`: `node scripts/check-database-first-legacy-stores.mjs`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check scripts/check-database-first-legacy-stores.mjs test/scripts/check-database-first-legacy-stores.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
